pages:
Welcome to the experiment.

In this experiment you will be presented with a series of words that progress automatically.
You will need to remember the sequence of words to complete a '2-back' task.
On each trial, you need to decide whether the word is the same as the one two (2) words ago (${var.keys.target}) or not (${var.keys.nonTarget}).

Let's have a short practice. Each trial will present one word.
You must decide whether the word matches the one presented two (2) trials ago.

If the word matches, it is a target.

'2-back' means the word before the previous word.
E.g. In the sequence 'honey', 'jelly', 'honey', the second 'honey' is a 2-back target.

You will receive feedback after the block is finished.
Words can repeat, so try to remember the sequence not just recognise words.

pages.title: Ready to begin
pages.text: Click the button below to start the study in fullscreen mode.

instructions.endPages:
DO NOT CLOSE THIS WINDOW!
That concludes the experiment. 
Thank you very much for participating.

Please read the debrief form on the next screen, then click Continue.
The experiment will redirect to SONA and automatically apply your credits.

Do not close the window until you see a message from SONA that your credits have been awarded!

variables.blockIntroGenericNoPM: You will now complete the next set of 2-back trials.
variables.blockIntroGenericPM: You will now complete the next set of 2-back and PM trials.
variables.afterIntroGenericPM:
Remember:

Press '${var.keys.pm}' for items in the PM category.
Press '${var.keys.target}' when the word is the same as it was two (2) words ago.
Otherwise, press '${var.keys.nonTarget}'.

Press "continue" to begin the next block.
variables.afterIntroGenericPM3:
Remember:

Press '${var.keys.pm}' for items in the PM categories.
Press '${var.keys.target}' when the word is the same as it was two (2) words ago.
Otherwise, press '${var.keys.nonTarget}'.

Press "continue" to begin the next block.

values.cell1PreScreen:
In the following blocks you will also perform a task we call "PM" (Prospective Memory).

The PM task requires you to press the '${var.keys.pm}' key on trials where a word belongs to a certain category.
You will be told what categories require a PM response.

You need to remember to press '${var.keys.pm}' whenever you see a word belonging to those categories.
values.cell1Intro:
For the following blocks, the PM category is:

"${var.pmOrder.0.descriptor}"

Press '${var.keys.pm}' for items in this category.

You will continue to respond to the n-back task when words do not belong to the PM category.
values.cell1PostScreen:
Well done!

You will no longer be required to respond to ${var.pmOrder.0.categoryKey} words.

In the next set of trials, you will be given a new set of categories for the PM task.
values.cell2Intro:
For the following blocks, the PM categories are

"${var.pmOrder.1.descriptor}"
"${var.pmOrder.2.descriptor}"
"${var.pmOrder.3.descriptor}"

Press '${var.keys.pm}' for items in any of these categories.

You will continue to respond to the n-back task when words do not belong to the PM categories.
values.cell2PostScreen:
There will be no more PM trials in the experiment.
For the remaining trials, please complete the n-back task as usual.
You do not need to look for any word categories.
values.cell3Intro:
Press '${var.keys.target}' when the word is the same as it was two (2) words ago.
Otherwise, press '${var.keys.nonTarget}'.

Press "continue" to begin the next block.
values.cell3AfterIntro:

variables.blockIntro:
Press '${var.keys.target}' if the word is the same as it was two (2) words ago.
Otherwise, press '${var.keys.nonTarget}'.

beforeBlockScreens:
Trials will proceed automatically, but you must respond to each word.

The first two words cannot be targets, because there are no corresponding two-back trials.
Note that words may repeat, but only repeats of exactly two (2) words ago are targets.

blocks.label: Practice Block
afterBlockScreens:
Great work! You will now begin the experimental trials.

Blocks consist of 100 words that proceed automatically.
For each word, decide if it was the same as the word two (2) words ago.

You will be given plenty of breaks between blocks.
repeatAfterBlockScreens: Press continue when you are ready for the next set of trials.

blocks.label: Block 3
afterBlockScreens:
You are halfway through the experiment.
Take a short breath before continuing.
